openQuestions.text: A: How do we stop the WRITE phase asserting interpretive relationships/causes/consistency not present in tokens.json (prompt, style-guide, format-spec constraints)?
A: What mechanical validator extension catches prose fabrication — claims-to-token provenance, banned interpretive phrases, the focus-"consistent" cross-check?
B: Which extraction gaps force the writer to invent (focus default-off, a11y async half never called, single-page motion, contrast top-20 cap, gradient non-decomposition)?
B: How accurate are OKLCH clustering + L1-L4 stability gating + component/variant classification, and where do they corrupt fidelity?
C: Which resource-doc / prompt-template / format-spec requirements actively SOLICIT fabrication (mandatory "named principle", comparative framing, required philosophy paragraphs)?

D: What do the best external extractors (DesignMD, design-extract, extract-design-system, Superposition, project-wallace, Style Dictionary, Open Design, CSS Stats) do for extraction fidelity + anti-fabrication + DESIGN.md narrative, and what is borrowable?
RESERVED (never mark answered): What new angles, risks, or cross-track connections emerge as research broadens? Keep open to prevent early convergence.

carriedForwardOpenQuestions.text: Track E: Statically check writing_style_guide.md for all pattern instructions that can generate unverifiable prose — identify every 'must include' / 'required' clause that lacks a corresponding validate.ts check.

Track B: Catalog EVERY prose-claim category across the 17 DESIGN.md sections that has zero token binding — starting with brand personality adjectives (design_md_format.md §2), motion philosophy (design_md_format.md §10: 'Motion serves as confirmation, not decoration'), and content-voice tone descriptors (design_md_format.md §11) — all of which the style guide demands be evocative and named but none of which validate.ts checks.
RESERVED: emergent angles/risks (permanently open)

Track C: Extend the source-provenance sentinel concept to the cardinal-rules card — add a rule 'Every prose claim about a design property (focus consistency, depth theory, motion philosophy absence) cites a token-key path or is flagged as inference' to cardinal_rules_card.md checklist (line 33-44).

Track D: Interaction capture default (OFF) vs. a11y contract requirement — the system defaults extract.ts to skip interaction capture, yet §9 Accessibility Contract is a REQUIRED section. The default extraction mode guarantees the A11yTokens are incomplete. Should extraction warn/error when --no-interaction is default and the DESIGN.md will include Focus Indicators?

Track A-i: Audit all A11yTokens fields for default-fabrication — does minTouchTarget report 0/0 similarly when no interactive elements found? Does altTextCoverage.percentage return 100 on empty? (a11y-extract.ts:322-326, 224-249 — parallel bug pattern.)
Test whether default-on interaction capture changes extraction time enough to justify the off-default (current PER_ELEMENT_TIMEOUT=6s × MAX_ELEMENTS=50 = 5min worst case per page)
Quantify: on a real extraction (e.g. stripe.com), what % of DESIGN.md sections have zero-backing-token fields — run extract, count empty/null token fields, cross-reference with §requirements
Audit the WRITE-phase prompt: does it instruct the AI to note 'no data' or does it implicitly require content for every cell/field, forcing fabrication
Investigate whether the validate.ts script catches fabricated values or only checks format/structure

Analyze DTCG 2025.10 spec for which $type values map meaningfully to the 17 DESIGN.md sections — which types are structural anchors vs. which are supplementary, to build the minimum viable tokens.dtcg.json schema

Deep-fetch design-extract/designlang (github Manavarya09, 3.3k stars) — study exact coverage threshold algorithm, multi-platform emitter architecture, MCP server design, and how coverage-based election handles shared-foundation tokens across independent surfaces

Compare DesignMD's 9-section schema contracts section-by-section against sk-design-md-generator's 17-section contracts to identify where DesignMD's constrained sections prevent fabrication that our open-ended sections permit

Audit current cluster.ts stability classifier against a known fabrication case (e.g., a real tokens.json where a one-off gradient got classified L2) to quantify the page-coverage vs. element-frequency scoring imbalance with real numbers

A6 implementation: write the gradient stability classification in cluster.ts (classifyGradientStability) and the three new check functions with test fixtures for L4 shadow/gradient/component content in DESIGN.md prose
A12 hardening: enumerate all backtick-wrapped values in 10 production DESIGN.md files to measure the false-positive rate of strict-allowlist mode vs current heuristic mode
A7 calibration: run the banned-word check against 5 existing DESIGN.md outputs to tune the numeric-anchor window size and identify additional false-positive patterns
A10 consumer impact: audit all callers of validateDesignMd (CLI, report-gen.ts, proof.ts) to understand how dual-score output affects exit codes and report rendering
Check whether `childrenCount` in ElementStyle correctly counts only direct element children or is inflated by text-node children in the DOM collector
Cross-validate deltaE vs DE2000/CIEDE2000 — the ×100 scaling may not correspond to any standard perceptual metric; consider switching to culori's built-in deltaEOK
Benchmark cluster accuracy against a labeled ground-truth dataset of 5–10 design systems (Material, Ant, Tailwind UI, etc.)
Audit the DESIGN.md writer for how L2/L3 tokens are rendered — does it respect 'Subject to change' annotation or inline L3 tokens as if canonical?

Trace the AP-25 (Unnamed Principles) and AP-26 (Convention Assumption) anti-patterns: they currently ENFORCE the fabrication solicitation by defining the ABSENCE of fabricated narrative as an anti-pattern. Should they be rescoped or retired?

Audit the four gold-standard examples (stripe, vercel, linear, supabase DESIGN.md + tokens.json pairs) for frequency of fabricated philosophy vs. evidence-backed philosophy — how many named principles in the exemplars actually trace to token keys?

Investigate whether the prompt template's cardinal-rules numbering (1-6 currently) should be restructured so the anti-fabrication rule reads as the NEW rule 1 (strongest priming position) rather than appended as rule 7.
Check whether the validate.ts script could be extended to detect 'philosophy without evidence' — flag any narrative paragraph in a mandatory section that contains zero token-key citations.

Deepen: host-fetch W3C DTCG 2025.10 Editor's Draft for the full $type enumeration (color, dimension, shadow, typography, gradient, cubicBezier, transition, strokeStyle, border, duration — which ones map to our DesignTokens fields?), composite token referencing syntax ($id + $extensions), and group/alias resolution rules

Deepen: host-fetch design-extract repo for exact coverage election threshold semantics (is it pagesCoverage >= threshold or a more nuanced 'canonical page count' metric?), DTCG composite token $id referencing mechanics, and the CSS health audit + WCAG report card architecture which would complement our a11y validation
Fetch DesignMD (designmd.cc) actual 9-section schema to confirm exact section names and whether any require prose vs pure tables
Evaluate adding a pre-writer frequency-clustering pass to the dmdg extraction pipeline (input-side anti-fabrication)
Fetch Open Design (nexu-io) DESIGN.md output format to compare section structure against v2 spec
Prototype a 'gated philosophy' version of Section 1 where Named Principles are generated only when ≥3 supporting CSS values exist with frequency ≥10
Test whether converting Section 1 from prose to table-first reduces fabrication in a controlled A/B with the same extraction data

A-i follow-up: backfill captured:boolean across all a11y-extract.ts extractors (minTouchTarget, altTextCoverage, focusIndicator, reducedMotionSupport) and update A11yTokens type + downstream consumers
A11 follow-up: implement the structured-output field tables in the format spec and update validate.ts to enforce field presence (not free prose)

Cross-cutting: validate.ts checkContent currently warns on missing typography table and low color count but has no anchoring-density check — add a metric: % of sentences containing ≥1 token-traceable value
Cross-cutting: the DesignTokens type (types.ts) needs auditing for other nullable fields that lack captured sentinels — motionData, shadowData, darkMode, etc.
A8 follow-up: audit all § examples in writing_style_guide.md for external-system claims; rewrite as intra-system comparisons
A15 follow-up: implement the adversarial self-review as a scripted pre-validate step (or embed in validate.ts as a new checkAnchoring pass)
Audit the DESIGN.md writer (generate-design-md.ts or Claude Code prompt) against a populated SectionCoverageReport to confirm which sections currently get invented content when token sets are thin.

Verify that adding checkSectionCoverage to validate.ts doesn't break existing scoring — phantom-color and missing-section are already hard-fail critical types; section coverage should be a warning (not hard-fail) for thin sections and an optional hard-fail gate for empty sections.
Test gradient regex decomposition against real-world gradient strings (nested rgba, multiple backgrounds, legacy -webkit- prefixes, conic gradients) to establish parser reliability.
Benchmark multi-viewport DOM re-extraction cost — the crawl already opens/closes browser contexts per page; adding 3-5 viewport resizes may be marginal or substantial depending on page count.
Define the exact DTCG token schema for each of the 8 token categories (color, typography, shadow, radius, spacing, motion, gradient, component) with $type and $value shapes
Map each of the 17 DESIGN.md sections to deterministic-vs-AI classification with specific formatter function signatures
Design the semantic alias computation rules for primary/secondary/surface/text/accent roles from frequency+usage data
Prototype dtcg-wrap.ts against existing stripe/tokens.json to measure migration effort
deepen: quantify the var()-chain resolution gap — what % of cssVariables[] entries in real extractions (stripe, vercel examples) are currently unlinked to colorTokens due to var() indirection?
deepen: benchmark the occurrence-band pre-clustering against the current greedy perceptual-only approach on the gold-standard examples to measure token-count inflation/deflation
deepen: prototype the role-typed extraction on one example site and compare the resulting cssVariables[] array size and composition against the current flat output

Implement AP-29 and the prose-fidelity check function (checkProseFidelity) in validate.ts—specific regex patterns for banned adjectives, recommendation-without-disclaimer detection, named-principle presence.
Add sentinel generation to the prompt template and sentinel validation to validate.ts—define the exact `<!-- src: ... -->` format and proximity tolerance.
Wire data-driven section requirements into checkSectionCompleteness—requires tokens.json schema inspection to confirm motion/iconography/darkMode field paths.
Implement fidelity-audit.ts as a standalone validator script (or as a --prose flag on validate.ts) that runs the anti-pattern checks validate.ts currently cannot.
Test the OBSERVED/RECOMMENDED boundary through the full pipeline: extract → write with new template → validate with new data-driven checks—confirm no fabricated values survive.
Audit §9 writer prompt to enforce transcription-only of a11y-extract.ts measured data (no invention)
Implement nearestCompliantShade() OKLCh binary search in a11y-extract.ts or remediation.ts
Refactor extractFocusIndicator() to axe-style rule-based audit with violation counts
Add opentype.js font-metrics.ts for x-height/cap-height extraction from .woff2 URLs
deepen: protopyte the className/role pre-pass in a shadow branch of cluster.ts to measure the fidelity improvement over pure geometry on 3 benchmark sites

deepen: trace the WRITE phase LLM instruction for §11 to identify which prompt fragment causes invention when state diffs are null — the format spec says 'use - when state applies but was not observed' but the writer interprets 'required section' as 'must produce content'

deepen: map each geometric heuristic (Badge/Card/Hero/Nav) against the className+role already collected to quantify false-positive rate — run cluster.ts against a DESIGN.md of a known component library to measure misdetection

deepen: compare Anima's specific Auto Layout→Flexbox mapping algorithm against our collected flex/grid token set to design the aggregation schema — what Anima infers from Figma we can infer from computed CSS

deepen: prototype the multi-file manifest output against the existing stripe example — benchmark writer citation accuracy (can an agent locate 'stripe's primary brand color' faster in manifest vs flat?); measure with a citation-time micro-benchmark

deepen: map specific cssVariableName patterns from 4 example sites (stripe/vercel/linear/supabase) to proposed alias keys — prove the alias extraction would actually work on real CSS variable naming conventions

deepen: analyze DTCG 2025.10 $extensions field — could we embed our stability classification (infrastructure/system/campaign/content) as a DTCG-compliant extension rather than a custom stability field?
deepen: benchmark whether splitting WRITE into auto-render + AI-annotate actually reduces validate.ts failure rate on the four example sites (stripe, vercel, linear, supabase)
deepen: design the render-tables.ts output format for deterministic sections — which markdown table structure best serves both human readers and agent parsers
deepen: design the human-annotation delimiter convention — HTML comments vs frontmatter vs a sidecar YAML — for the merge-update preservation model
deepen: prototype the cite-the-token annotation format and validate.ts enhancement — what's the minimal change to make numeric citations machine-checkable

Deepen: Compare Open Design's deterministic re-render DESIGN.md schema section-by-section against our format spec to identify exactly which content-type constraints prevent fabrication in each section

Deepen: If Open Design's DESIGN.md output format is publicly documented in their repo, extract the exact 9-section schema contract (section names, content types, constraints) for direct comparison with our design_md_format.md sections

Deepen: Run a controlled experiment: generate DESIGN.md with current WRITE prompt vs with evidence-quota-augmented prompt vs with vibe-paragraph-removed prompt, then measure hex fidelity AND prose-accuracy against ground-truth tokens

Deepen: Benchmark the 4 gold-standard example DESIGN.md outputs against their source tokens.json to quantify: (a) how many atmosphere claims are supported by >=3 token values, (b) how many named principles have zero token evidence, (c) whether any Section 7 vibe-paragraph claims contradict observed UI text

Deepen: Study the SKILL.md prompt architecture of extract-design-system — how does its skill prompt structure (phase detection, resource loading, cardinal rules) compare to ours? Is its prompt shorter/more constrained/less interpretive?
Verify B13 fix does not introduce false-positive interaction matches across unrelated components (current fallback class-substring direction can over-match)
Check if other files (writer/MD generator) import the exported classifyShadow and would break if its signature changes during dedup
The cluster.ts a11y computation (lines 1506-1568) produces focusIndicator/contrastPairs/minTouchTarget/minFontSize that is always discarded — ~60 lines of dead code that should be removed
Audit interactionLookup dedup: only one capture per first-class-prefix is stored — could two variants share the same prefix and overwrite each other's interaction data?
Prototype canvas gradient sampler for dom-collector.ts to validate pixel-sampling accuracy vs CSS-string parsing
Implement selectorAnchors on ColorToken + structural-region confidence signal (P0+P1 scope, cluster.ts + types.ts)
Audit whether low-confidence tokens currently leak into L1/L2 DESIGN.md sections by inspecting gold-standard examples (stripe, vercel)
Design .values.json schema and measure context-token reduction vs DESIGN.md for agent consumption

Ablation study: generate two DESIGN.md files from the same tokens.json — one with full narrative (current) and one with value-tables-only (fabrication-safe). Compare hex accuracy, role assignment, and information density
Test the dynamic OKLCH threshold (chroma-scaled) against the fixed deltaE<3 threshold on the gold-standard examples to quantify merge accuracy
Port color_role_taxonomy.md's identification signals into a deterministic classifyColorRole() in cluster.ts and measure agreement with gold-standard DESIGN.md role assignments

Calibrate L1-L4 thresholds against known design systems (Material Design, Polaris, Atlassian) — run the stability classifier on their tokens.json and measure precision/recall against published token catalogs

deepen: DTCG $description field taxonomy — define description templates per $type (color: frequency+role+contrast; dimension: scale-position+usage+responsive; shadow: level+technique+component) so auto-generated prose is parameterized, not invented
deepen: DESIGN.md claim-verification protocol — for each of 17 sections, specify which DTCG $type(s) a claim must cite; implement in validate.ts as DTCG-conformance gate
deepen: prototype DTCG token schema adapter for extract.ts — map current flat tokens.json fields to DTCG $type/$value/$description/composite structures
Benchmark deterministic-tables vs AI-written tables: run controlled experiment on 4 gold-standard examples measuring hex fidelity + prose accuracy
Prototype dtcg-wrap.ts against stripe/tokens.json to measure DTCG migration effort and validate type-gated validation reduces failure rate
Audit whether low-confidence tokens leak into L1/L2 DESIGN.md sections in gold-standard examples (iter-020 P1 confidence-gate gap)
Design .values.json schema from iter-020 P1 and measure context-token reduction vs DESIGN.md for agent consumption (target: ~70% reduction)

Audit the WRITE-phase prompt template (design_md_prompt_template.md) for all clauses that demand content when data is absent — catalog every 'must include' / 'required' that lacks a 'when absent, stamp ABSENT' fallback

Prototype the inline `<!-- source: tokens.x -->` sentinel mechanism: parse sentinels from raw MD via stripHtmlComments path, resolve dotted paths against DesignTokens, verify non-empty/non-null — measure false-positive rate on 3 real DESIGN.md outputs
A/B test: generate DESIGN.md with current mandatory-philosophy prompt vs evidence-gated philosophy prompt (same tokens.json) — measure fabrication rate per section
Implement coverage-election as additive pre-gate in cluster.ts, run against existing stripe/vercel tokens.json to quantify L2→L3 demotion count
study whether L2/L3 boundary tokens are the highest-fabrication-risk class (campaign tokens have thin evidence but are allowed with caveats)
deepen per-section evidence-level computation (which dimensions map to which DESIGN.md sections, what thresholds define FULL/PARTIAL/ABSENT/FABRICATED)
spec the evidence-level output schema for the validator enhancement
Audit design_md_prompt_template.md for ALL clauses demanding content when data is absent — catalog every 'must include' without ABSENT fallback
A/B test: evidence-gated named-principle prompt vs current mandatory-philosophy prompt — measure fabrication rate per section
Prototype inline source sentinel mechanism: parse sentinels from raw MD, resolve dotted paths against DesignTokens, measure false-positive rate on 3 real DESIGN.md outputs
Backfill captured:boolean across all a11y-extract.ts extractors and audit DesignTokens for other nullable fields lacking sentinels
Implement data-driven section requirements in checkSectionCompleteness — requires tokens.json schema inspection to confirm field paths for each conditional section
Benchmark default-on interaction capture extraction time (PER_ELEMENT_TIMEOUT=6s × MAX_ELEMENTS=50 = 5min worst case per page) to determine if opt-in was justified
Audit DESIGN.md writer prompt: does it instruct AI to note 'no data' or implicitly require content for every cell/field, forcing fabrication when backing tokens are empty
Benchmark multi-viewport DOM re-extraction cost — crawl already opens/closes browser contexts per page; adding 3-5 viewport resizes may be marginal or substantial
Quantify on real extraction (e.g. stripe.com): what % of DESIGN.md sections have zero-backing-token fields — run extract, count empty/null token fields, cross-reference with section requirements
Verify B13 fix (componentType key in findInteraction) does not introduce false-positive interaction matches across unrelated components with same tag+class prefix
Test gradient regex decomposition against real-world gradient strings (nested rgba, multiple backgrounds, legacy -webkit- prefixes, conic gradients) to establish parser reliability

Trace AP-25 (Unnamed Principles) and AP-26 (Convention Assumption) anti-patterns — they currently ENFORCE fabrication solicitation by defining the ABSENCE of fabricated narrative as an error. Should they be rescoped or retired?

Audit the four gold-standard examples (stripe/vercel/linear/supabase DESIGN.md + tokens.json pairs) for: (a) how many named principles actually trace to token keys, (b) how many atmosphere claims have ≥3 backing token values, (c) whether any vibe-paragraph claims contradict observed UI text
Re-scope cardinal rules numbering: should the anti-fabrication rule read as rule 1 (strongest priming position) rather than appended?
Implement the ABSENT-stamp mechanism end-to-end: apply to all 17 sections in format spec, wire into prompt template cardinal rules, and add to ESCALATE IF routing path
Benchmark the prompt template with evidence-gated philosophy vs. current unconditional prompt against the same tokens.json to measure fabrication rate per section
Audit WRITE-phase prompt template for all 'must include' clauses lacking 'when absent, stamp ABSENT' fallback
Implement coverage-election pre-gate in cluster.ts, run against gold-standard examples to quantify L2→L3 demotion count
Prototype DTCG parallel output (tokens.dtcg.json) against stripe/tokens.json to measure migration effort
A/B test: generate DESIGN.md with current mandatory-philosophy prompt vs evidence-gated prompt — measure fabrication rate per section

Design DTCG $description taxonomy per $type (color: frequency+role+contrast; dimension: scale-position+usage+responsive; shadow: level+technique+component) so auto-generated prose from $description fields is parameterized, not invented
Benchmark Phase A (only §2+§3 auto-rendered) vs full Phase B against 4 gold-standard examples: measure hex fidelity, weight/lineHeight/shadow value accuracy, and validation score delta
Prototype formatters.ts against stripe/tokens.json to measure: (a) exact % of DESIGN.md lines replaced, (b) end-to-end hex fidelity improvement, (c) context-token reduction for agent consumers

Design the citation syntax for checkCitationGating(): markdown-compatible format that survives rendering but is parseable by validate.ts — options: HTML comment (<!-- token: ... -->), backtick annotation (`token:...`), or reference-style links ([^token-1])
Audit whether low-confidence tokens (cluster.ts confidence < 0.5) leak into gold-standard DESIGN.md examples — confirms the need for pre-render confidence gating in formatters.ts
Prototype formatters.ts against stripe/tokens.json — measure exact % of DESIGN.md lines replaced, end-to-end hex fidelity improvement, context-token reduction for agent consumers
Audit design_md_prompt_template.md for ALL clauses demanding content when data absent — catalog every 'must include' lacking ABSENT fallback
Implement coverage-election pre-gate in cluster.ts, run against gold-standard examples — quantify L2→L3 demotion count
A/B test evidence-gated philosophy prompt vs current mandatory prompt against same tokens.json — measure fabrication rate per section
Benchmark default-on interaction capture extraction time against real sites to confirm opt-out is viable for speed-critical runs
Design DTCG $description taxonomy per $type so auto-generated prose from $description fields is parameterized, not invented
Quantify: run the prevalence gate on a real 5-page extraction to measure how many colorMap entries it eliminates vs. how many system tokens it preserves

Gradient stops: extractGradientColors (cluster.ts:244-261) feeds colors into the same colorMap — should gradient-derived colors have a separate prevalence threshold since gradients inherently use unique stop colors?
Edge case: CSS-variable-defined colors (e.g., --gray-500: #646464) that appear on 1 page — should CSS var presence override the page-count gate?

deepen: extraction feasibility — can the DOM collector reliably decompose box-shadow strings into {color, offsetX, offsetY, blur, spread} given browser-computed style variations (rgba vs rgb vs hex, inset keyword, multiple shadows)?

deepen: author a ReferenceCompositeToken type that wraps a composite value + alias label + provenance (CSS variable or DOM source) as a generic pattern for TypographyRole, ColorRole, BorderToken, ShadowToken

deepen: format-spec impact — if tokens carry typed aliases, does design_md_format.md §2 color naming rule ('Descriptive brand-specific name, never Blue 1') change to 'Use CSS variable-derived role name when available; invent only when no variable exists'?
deepen: quantify fabrication reduction — count how many invented strings (role names, color names, personality sentences) per DESIGN.md section with current vs composite pipeline
Audit WRITE-phase prompt for all 'must include' clauses that lack 'when absent, stamp ABSENT' — cross-reference against iteration-029 items 4+7 (evidence-gating + confidence surfacing)

Prototype freshness stamp: run proof.ts on a gold-standard extraction (stripe), modify tokens.meta.freshness, re-run proof.ts after simulating site change — measure coverage delta threshold for STALE classification
Implement extractionCount in mergeTokenSets types + merge logic, run --merge-with against two sequential extractions of the same site to verify frequency accumulation correctness
Map the DTCG alias syntax ($value="{path.to.token}") to DESIGN.md citation anchors—how the validator verifies §3 table rows cite the correct TypographyLevel.id
Spec the DTCGView projection interface in types.ts—exact TypeScript types for the typed alias layer
Design the id generation algorithm: which canonical properties per token kind produce stable, collision-resistant hashes
Prototype the $type classifier for ShadowToken: parse 'rgba(0,0,0,0.04) 0px 1px 2px 0px' into {color, offsetX, offsetY, blur, spread} for auto-level-assignment
Verify that the cluster.ts duplicate a11y computation (lines 1547-1568) is genuinely dead — trace the call chain from extract.ts to confirm overwrite
Benchmark interaction-ON default against real-world sites (Vercel, Stripe) — confirm 30s/page budget holds

Audit design_md_prompt_template.md for ALL clauses demanding content when data absent — the plan identifies format spec mandates but the prompt template may have additional fabrication-forcing clauses
Prototype formatters.ts against stripe/tokens.json — the architecture claim that this works is currently unverified against real extracted tokens
Run deltaE threshold change against all 4 gold-standard examples + anobel — measure color palette delta before/after
Build a token-coverage audit: for each section, count how many claims have grounding tokens vs how many are pure inference
Implement the anti-fabrication ruleset as a validator that checks each section against its rules before publishing
Create a writer contract: for each section, define the exact prose template that enforces the grounding rules (e.g. §1 opening must embed 2+ tokens)
Catalog the specific banned comparative framings (the 'unlike most systems' corpus problem) and propose a grounded alternative template
Benchmark default-on interaction capture extraction time against real sites to confirm opt-out viability
Prototype formatters.ts against stripe/tokens.json — measure % of DESIGN.md lines replaced, hex fidelity improvement, context-token reduction
Design DTCG $description taxonomy per $type so auto-generated prose is parameterized not invented

iter-44: AUDIT UN-INSPECTED SCRIPTS — Audit icon-detect.ts, framework-detect.ts, dark-mode-detect.ts, and crawl.ts for the 'fabricated-default-on-empty-data' bug class (parallel to focus-indicator pattern).
iter-43: EMPIRICAL CLOSING WAVE PART 3 — A/B test evidence-gated vs mandatory-philosophy prompt on 2 examples. Implement+quantify coverage-election pre-gate against gold-standard tokens.json.

iter-40: EXECUTE PROMPT TEMPLATE ABSENT-FALLBACK AUDIT — the most-repeated open question. Read design_md_prompt_template.md line-by-line, catalog every 'must include' without fallback. Resolve §9 vs §6.5 demotion inconsistency.
iter-41: EMPIRICAL CLOSING WAVE PART 1 — Run deltaE<10 threshold change against all 4 gold-standard examples + anobel. Measure color token count delta + brand color loss. Update cluster.test.ts.
iter-42: EMPIRICAL CLOSING WAVE PART 2 — Prototype formatters.ts Phase A (§2 Colors + §3 Typography) against stripe/tokens.json. Measure auto-generated vs AI-written line ratio, value accuracy delta.
Add an ABSENT-stamp and inference-label validator to validate.ts (or a companion prose-fidelity check script)
Implement the cardinal-rules rewrite (Rules 7-10) into design_md_prompt_template.md and cardinal_rules_card.md, keeping cardinal_rules_card.md as the machine-readable mirror
Audit the EXTRACT-phase prompt template (extraction_prompt.md) for the same fabrication-gap classes (unconstrained prose, missing ABSENT stamps, unlabeled inference)
Dry-run the rewritten prompt against a deliberately gapped token set (e.g., zero motion, zero ARIA, only 3 colors) and measure section-level ABSENT stamp rate vs. invention rate
Check if icon-detect's colorMode:'mixed' on empty data has caused incorrect §12 Iconography output in existing DESIGN.md files
Audit motion-extract.ts (not in scope but same extraction pipeline) for the same pattern
Check report-gen.ts and the DESIGN.md template writer: how does it handle empty variableDiff when darkMode.supported is true? Does it fabricate palettes?
Verify whether the §2.5 dark-mode section in the actual DESIGN.md template has a conditional gate on variableDiff.length
Consider a unified 'no-data sentinel' type (e.g., { _empty: true }) across all extractors to prevent writers from inventing data

Check whether any existing examples (stripe/vercel/linear/supabase DESIGN.md files) contain fabricated values vs. their matching tokens.json — this quantifies the fabrication rate in the current manual pipeline.
Audit tool/resources/design_md_format.md for the v2 section specification — what are the exact 17 section names and their required fields? The prompt-builder hardcodes or reads these.
Trace how validate.ts's section-completeness check maps to DESIGN.md section names — does it validate by heading regex? This determines which section names the prompt-builder must emit.
Design the prompt-builder's output format: what does the constrained WRITE prompt look like (section-by-section mockup of pre-rendered tables + prose zones + DATA_AVAILABILITY blocks)?
Design the builder's integration with the existing TypeScript types (types.ts: DesignTokens) so it can consume tokens.json with full type safety.
Measure deltaE<10 vs <3 clustering on the same tokens (deferred — needs cluster.ts harness)
Benchmark interaction capture wall-clock time on 3 real-world sites (JS-heavy, CSS-heavy, static) to confirm 15s PAGE_TIMEOUT is sufficient
Verify that removing dead-code clusterTokens a11y (lines 1506-1568) doesn't break the mergeTokenSets path (cluster.ts:1648+) which may read a11yTokens from clusterTokens output
Cross-validate deltaE<8 on a golden dataset of 10 design systems to confirm no functional-color merge regressions
Verify classifyByTagRole parity with classifyComponent for ALL tag/role combos including implicit roles (a/button/input without role attr)
Red-team research.md itself; pre-mortem the Phase-1 ship; deltaE<3-vs-<10 empirical (deferred).
Calibrate the deltaE threshold claim: measure color-token counts before/after deltaE <3 vs <10 on the existing examples to quantify merge risk.
Verify the regression-guard claims: do the 4 gold-standard examples (Stripe, Vercel, Linear, Supabase) actually exist with tokens.json at the claimed paths in tool/examples/?
Verify the `interaction-capture.ts` componentType derivation path to confirm or refute the classifyComponent() attribution.
Test whether the 'ABSENT-stamp' pattern (proposed B6) is actually in the design_md_format.md §22 or whether §22 exists at all — the citation design_md_format.md §22 is unverified.
Enumerate the exact banned-adjective list and test against all 4 gold-standard DESIGN.md files for false positives
Prototype deltaE threshold at 5/7/10 and measure merge quality on Tailwind/Material/Vercel palettes
Run gold-standard extractions with interaction ON to measure actual time delta and token diff
Audit cluster.ts:1506-1568 dead-code a11y path — confirm extract.ts:469 overwrite covers all fields or delete the duplicate
Define the SectionDataMap type with exact DesignTokens dot-paths for each conditional section
Re-measure deltaE on the 4 gold-standard examples to set a defensible per-corpus threshold (future packet).

RESERVED: emergent angles/risks (permanently open — the loop ends by cap, not exhaustion).

uncoveredQuestions: A: How do we stop the WRITE phase asserting interpretive relationships/causes/consistency not present in tokens.json (prompt, style-guide, format-spec constraints)?
A: What mechanical validator extension catches prose fabrication — claims-to-token provenance, banned interpretive phrases, the focus-"consistent" cross-check?
B: Which extraction gaps force the writer to invent (focus default-off, a11y async half never called, single-page motion, contrast top-20 cap, gradient non-decomposition)?
B: How accurate are OKLCH clustering + L1-L4 stability gating + component/variant classification, and where do they corrupt fidelity?
C: Which resource-doc / prompt-template / format-spec requirements actively SOLICIT fabrication (mandatory "named principle", comparative framing, required philosophy paragraphs)?

D: What do the best external extractors (DesignMD, design-extract, extract-design-system, Superposition, project-wallace, Style Dictionary, Open Design, CSS Stats) do for extraction fidelity + anti-fabrication + DESIGN.md narrative, and what is borrowable?
RESERVED (never mark answered): What new angles, risks, or cross-track connections emerge as research broadens? Keep open to prevent early convergence.

keyFindings.text: **[P0] depth-principle-solicited-no-binding** — Two format authorities conspire to demand an unverifiable named principle: (A) design_md_format.md §9 (Depth & Elevation, line 544) REQUIRES a 'Named principle — state the depth philosophy upfront using one of these categories (or a custom name)'. The 'or a custom name' clause explicitly authorizes invention. (B) writing_style_guide.md §16 (Named Principle Pattern, lines 589-631) provides Pattern B '[technique]-as-[metaphor]' with 10+ examples cross-referenced from the Shadow Philosophy Pattern (§12) — together these solicit 'gradient-as-depth', 'chromatic-depth', 'shadow-as-border' as the expected voice. The AI, obligated to produce a named principle, invents 'gradient-as-depth' even when tokens.gradients[] is empty or has zero depth-relevant entries. validate.ts' 6 checks never read §6 prose for truth against tokens.shadowTokens[] or tokens.gradients[].

**[P0] focus-consistent-fabrication** — extractFocusIndicator returns {consistent:true} when focusStyles.length===0, i.e. when NO interaction data was captured. Interaction capture is OFF by default (extract.ts:191: '--no-interaction … default: skipped'; --fast forces it off). The empty style + true boolean survives through to DESIGN.md §9 Focus Indicators, where the AI writes 'focus indicator is consistent' with zero evidence. validate.ts has NO check that reads the Accessibility Contract prose against a11yTokens.focusIndicator.

**[P1] claims-to-token-provenance-gap** — All 6 validate.ts checks are mechanical: hex-tracing (phantom-color, stability-gating), font-name-tracing (unknown-fonts), format linting (format-consistency), section heading counting (section-completeness), and lightweight content inspection (content-checks: typography table existence, color count threshold). NONE reads a prose SENTENCE to verify the claim it makes. Both known hallucinations ('focus indicator is consistent', 'gradient-as-depth replaces shadow elevation') passed the 99/100 score because no check maps a prose assertion to a token entry. The WRITE phase has no mechanism to declare which token(s) back each claim, so validation cannot cross-check.

Evidence: a11y-extract.ts:101-103 (returns consistent:true on empty array); extract.ts:191 (interaction capture skipped by default); validate.ts:318-350 (6 checks, none inspecting focus prose); design_md_format.md §13 Focus Indicators (line 796-803, prose format section); types.ts:271-274 (A11yTokens.focusIndicator.consistent: boolean with no captured flag)

Evidence: design_md_format.md §9:534-575 (named principle REQUIRED, line 544 authorizes custom names); writing_style_guide.md §16:589-631 (Pattern B solicits 'technique-as-metaphor'); writing_style_guide.md §12:473-487 (Shadow Philosophy Pattern reinforces naming); validate.ts:318-350 (zero depth-prose checks); tokens.json DesignTokens.type:394-399 (gradients array — empty when no gradients extracted)

Evidence: validate.ts:51-53 (stripHtmlComments exists); validate.ts:318-350 (6 checks, zero sentence-level verification); cardinal_rules_card.md:33-44 (fidelity checklist has zero prose-verification items); design_md_prompt_template.md:40-56 (cardinal rules mention only value-copy fidelity, not claim-to-token binding)

Recommendation: Adopt INLINE `<!-- source: tokens.x -->` sentinels over a claims.json sidecar. Rationale: (a) validate.ts already has `stripHtmlComments()` at line 51-53 — stripping is an existing, working path; (b) single-file provenance — no sidecar to desync; (c) the sentinel lives adjacent to the claim it grounds, making manual review possible. Implementation: (1) WRITE-phase prompt must embed `<!-- source: tokens.<path> -->` before every claim-containing sentence (e.g., `<!-- source: tokens.a11yTokens.focusIndicator -->`). (2) Add `checkSourceProvenance(md, tokens)` to validate.ts that: parses all html comments in raw md (before stripHtmlComments); extracts `source: tokens.<path>` entries; resolves each dotted path against the DesignTokens object; verifies the referenced key exists and its value is non-empty/non-null; fails any claim whose source path leads to an empty array, zero-length object, or falsy key that is semantically required (e.g., `consistent:false` without `captured:true`). This would have caught BOTH cases: the focus claim would reference `tokens.a11yTokens.focusIndicator.style` which is empty `{}`, and the gradient-depth claim would reference `tokens.gradients[]` which would be empty `[]`.

Recommendation: THREE changes: (1) In a11y-extract.ts:88-103, change the zero-length branch to return `{ style: {}, consistent: false, captured: false }` — add a `captured` field to A11yTokens.focusIndicator (types.ts:271-274). (2) In validate.ts, add a new function `checkA11yFocusProvidence(md, tokens)` called from `validateDesignMd` that: locates the §9 Focus Indicators subsection text; scans for prose describing focus styles or using 'consistent'; if any exists, gates on `tokens.a11yTokens.focusIndicator.captured===true && Object.keys(tokens.a11yTokens.focusIndicator.style).length>0`. If captured===false, ANY focus description beyond 'Not observed' is a failure. (3) If the word 'consistent' appears, additionally require `consistent===true` vs `===false` — but with the gate above, consistent can only be true when there ARE focus styles (the new code only sets consistent true when styles exist).

Recommendation: TWO changes: (1) In validate.ts, add function `checkDepthPrincipleBinding(md, tokens)` that: extracts the named principle from §6 prose (first bold phrase after 'Named principle' or first bold term in the section); maps principle keywords to token requirements — 'gradient' → `tokens.gradients.length > 0` else FAIL; 'shadow[-as]' → `tokens.shadowTokens.length > 0` else FAIL; 'chromatic' → at least one `shadowTokens[]` value contains non-neutral RGBA (e.g., not pure (0,0,0,x) or (0,0,0,x)); for ANY named principle, require at least one `shadowToken.type==='elevation'` or FAIL with 'principle references depth but no elevation-type shadows found'. (2) In design_md_format.md §9 line 544, change '(or a custom name)' to '(or a custom name derived from and traceable to a specific token entry)', and add a sub-rule: 'A named principle MUST be traceable to a concrete token entry via an inline source comment or validator-verifiable pattern.'

**[P0] B1: Interaction capture OFF by default** — Confirmed. `extract.ts:81` sets `noInteraction = true` as default; `--fast` (line 118-119) forces it off. When off, `captureInteractions` is never called (line 322), so `interactionSets` is empty. This nullifies: (a) all component variant state changes (hoverDiff, focusVisibleDiff, focusDiff, activeDiff, disabledStyle) in cluster.ts:1347-1351, (b) focusIndicator.style stays empty `{}` (a11y-extract.ts:101-103), (c) loadingStates/emptyStates/errorStates never populated. §11 State Matrix requires 4+ components × 5 state columns — entirely fabricated without interaction data. §4 Component Stylings requires hover/focus/active/disabled lines with rationale — all null. §9 Focus Indicators has empty style object.

**[P0] B2: extractA11yAsync never called — 5 §9 fields always null** — Confirmed. `extract.ts:445` calls `extractA11y(domCollections, interactionSets)` — the sync version. The async variant `extractA11yAsync` (a11y-extract.ts:361-391) which returns `tabOrder`, `langAttribute`, `skipLinkDetected`, `altTextCoverage` is exported but never imported or called from extract.ts. §9 Accessibility Contract requires: ARIA patterns (OK from ariaRoleStats), contrast ratios (OK), focus indicators (empty without B1), touch targets (OK), reduced-motion support (always undefined — see B2a), lang attribute (never populated), skip link (never populated), alt text coverage (never populated). The AI invents all five missing fields.

**[P0] B2a: cssAnalyses not passed to extractA11y — reducedMotionSupport always undefined** — Confirmed, compounding B2. `extractA11y` signature accepts optional `cssAnalyses` parameter (a11y-extract.ts:260). `extract.ts:445` passes only `(domCollections, interactionSets)` — omits `cssAnalyses`. The `extractReducedMotionSupport` helper (a11y-extract.ts:213-220) checks for `prefers-reduced-motion` media breakpoints but is never reached. §9 'Reduced-Motion Support' subsection and §6.5 'Reduced-Motion Fallback' both require this data. Always undefined → AI fabricates a recommendation instead of reporting observed behavior.

**[P1] B5: colorRelationships.contrastPairs capped at top-20 colors (§2, not §9)** — Partially confirmed, scope correction needed. `cluster.ts:799` filters to top-20 highest-frequency colors (`frequency >= 5`, then `.slice(0, 20)`), then computes pairwise contrast among those 20. This populates `tokens.colorRelationships.contrastPairs`. However, `extract.ts:469` OVERWRITES `tokens.a11yTokens` with the output of `extractA11y`, which computes contrast pairs from actual DOM element color/backgroundColor pairs WITHOUT a cap (a11y-extract.ts:265-304). So §9 a11y contrast data is NOT capped — it comes from extractA11y. The cap affects `colorRelationships.contrastPairs` which informs §2 Color Palette color relationship data. Real text-on-bg pairs involving lower-frequency colors are missed in the colorRelationships view.

**[P1] B7: Motion extracted from first page CSS only** — Confirmed. `extract.ts:439` passes `cssAnalyses[0]` to `extractMotion` — only the first page's CSS analysis. `extractMotion` (motion-extract.ts:95-173) iterates `cssAnalysis.transitions` and `cssAnalysis.animations` from that single analysis. If page 2+ has unique keyframe animations or distinct timing functions, they are discarded. §6.5 Motion System requires duration scale, easing functions, and keyframe animations — thin data forces the AI to invent a 'motion philosophy' from incomplete observations.

**[P1] NEW: §0/§1/§7/§8 have zero extraction tokens — pure interpretation** — Four required sections have NO backing token fields: §0 Brand Context (identity, audience, personality), §1 Visual Theme & Atmosphere (design principles, comparative framing), §7 Content & Voice (tone, capitalization, button labels, error copy), §8 Do's and Don'ts (implementation rules). The extraction provides raw CSS values but zero semantic/interpretive data for these sections. The AI must invent ALL prose, adjectives, tone descriptors, and rules from raw hex/px/font tokens. These sections are the highest fabrication risk after §11 because they require subjective interpretation with no data guardrails.

**[P1] NEW: §11 State Matrix — global states not linked to components** — Even when interaction capture IS enabled, `interaction-capture.ts:394-690` detects loading/empty/error states as GLOBAL page patterns (class-based and ARIA-based), not per-component. `InteractionData.loadingStates/emptyStates/errorStates` are flat arrays with no component-type binding. `cluster.ts:1283-1303` links interaction CAPTURES to components by tag+classes, but loading/empty/error states are not linked. §11 requires a component×state matrix (Button×Loading, Card×Empty, etc.) — the AI must fabricate which global state pattern maps to which component.

**[P2] B6: Gradients stored as raw strings — no structural decomposition** — Confirmed. `types.ts:124-130` GradientInfo stores `value` as a raw CSS string (e.g. `linear-gradient(135deg, #667eea 0%, #764ba2 100%)`). `cluster.ts:1449-1483` aggregates gradients with `type`, `value`, `elementTag`, `location` — no parsing of angle, stop positions, or stop colors into structured fields. §6 Depth & Elevation 'Decorative Depth' subsection and §2 gradient endpoint colors require parsed data. The AI writer must regex-parse raw gradient strings to extract angles and stops, enabling angle/stop fabrication.

**[P2] NEW: §10 Responsive collapsing strategy has no extraction backing** — Breakpoints are extracted from CSS media queries across all pages (OK). But §10 'Collapsing Strategy' requires observing how specific elements change size/layout across breakpoints (e.g. 'Hero headline: 56px → 44px at 1024px'). The extraction collects elements at a single viewport (1440×900, extract.ts:298) per page — no multi-viewport element comparison. The AI must fabricate all pixel-transition claims.

**[P2] NEW: §12 Iconography — icon-to-text alignment not extracted** — Icon detection (icon-detect.ts) provides library, sizeScale, strokeWidth, totalCount, colorMode. But §12 requires 'Icon-to-Text Alignment' (gap values, vertical centering method). No extraction of icon-adjacent-element spacing exists. The AI fabricates alignment claims from DOM structure alone.
Evidence: cluster.ts:799 (slice cap), a11y-extract.ts:265-304 (uncapped DOM pairs), extract.ts:469 (overwrite)
Evidence: design_md_format.md §0 (lines 74-101), §1 (104-147), §7 (647-726), §8 (729-762) — no corresponding token fields in types.ts DesignTokens
Evidence: design_md_format.md §12 lines 952-958 (alignment requirement), icon-detect.ts (no adjacency analysis)
Evidence: extract.ts:298 (single viewport 1440×900), design_md_format.md §10 lines 866-871 (requires exact pixel transitions)
Evidence: extract.ts:439 (`cssAnalyses[0]` only), motion-extract.ts:102-111 (iterates single analysis)
Evidence: extract.ts:445 (missing 3rd arg), a11y-extract.ts:260 (optional param), a11y-extract.ts:345-347 (conditional logic never reached)
Evidence: extract.ts:445 (sync call only), a11y-extract.ts:361-391 (async fn unused), a11y-extract.ts:148-249 (async helpers exist but unreachable)
Evidence: extract.ts:81 (default true), extract.ts:117-119 (--fast forces off), extract.ts:321-328 (conditional call)

Evidence: interaction-capture.ts:394-487 (global detection), types.ts:177-199 (no component binding), cluster.ts:1283-1303 (capture-to-component linking exists for InteractionCapture but not for state detections)
Evidence: types.ts:124-130 (raw value field), cluster.ts:1476-1481 (no decomposition)

Recommendation: Add a gradient parser to cluster.ts that decomposes `value` into `{ angle: number, stops: { color: string, position: number }[] }`. Extend `GradientInfo` type in types.ts with structured fields. Use the same color parser (`parseColor`) already available in cluster.ts.

Recommendation: Call `extractA11yAsync` inside the per-page extraction loop (extract.ts:303-336), merge results into `a11yTokens` after the sync call. Requires passing `page` to the async call. Alternatively, move the page-dependent extractions (tabOrder, langAttribute, skipLink, altText) into the existing Playwright page context before `context.close()` at line 334.

Recommendation: Default `noInteraction` to `false` (opt-out instead of opt-in). Remove the `noInteraction = true` from `--fast` or add `--fast-with-interaction` escape hatch. At minimum, always capture a lightweight pass (focus + disabled only, skip hover/active) to guarantee minimum §11/§9 data.

Recommendation: During `detectLoadingStates`/`detectEmptyStates`/`detectErrorStates`, also capture the parent component context (nearest interactive ancestor's tag/role). Add a `componentType` field to `LoadingStateInfo`/`EmptyStateInfo`/`ErrorStateInfo`. Or post-hoc in cluster.ts: match state detection selectors against identified component elements.

Recommendation: For §7: Extract button label text from DOM (`textContent` already collected per element in ElementStyle). Extract heading text for tone analysis. For §0: Extract `<meta description>` and `<title>` from crawled pages. For §1/§8: These are inherently interpretive — add a 'confidence annotation' requirement so the AI marks low-confidence claims. Consider making §8 optional or data-derived (auto-generate Do/Don't from detected patterns).

Recommendation: Increase the cap or change the filter. Either raise `.slice(0, 20)` to `.slice(0, 50)` or filter by `frequency >= 2` instead of `>= 5`. Also consider using the actual DOM-derived pairs (from extractA11y) for colorRelationships instead of the synthetic pairwise computation.

Recommendation: Low priority. Icon-to-text gap can be inferred from DOM element rects (icon element + adjacent text element gap). Add a post-processing step in cluster.ts or icon-detect.ts that finds SVG elements with adjacent text siblings and computes the gap.

Recommendation: Merge all `cssAnalyses` before passing to `extractMotion`: create a combined CSSAnalysis with concatenated transitions/animations arrays from all pages. Or loop `extractMotion` per-page and merge the resulting MotionSystem objects (deduplicating by animation name).
Recommendation: Pass `cssAnalyses` as third argument: `extractA11y(domCollections, interactionSets, cssAnalyses)` at extract.ts:445. Single-line fix.

Recommendation: Run DOM collection at 2-3 viewport sizes (1440, 768, 375) per page and diff element rect/fontSize across viewports. This is expensive but the only way to produce real collapsing data. Alternatively, parse CSS media query rules for responsive font-size/padding declarations and map them to element selectors.

**[P0] Coverage-based token election (design-extract/designlang)** — design-extract crawls multiple canonical pages and ELECTS tokens by site-wide page-coverage, not just element frequency. A token appearing on only 1 of 8 pages is excluded regardless of how many elements use it on that page. The current L1-L4 classifier in cluster.ts uses additive scoring across 5 signals (pageCoverage, usage dimensions, CSS var presence, frequency, chromaticity) — a one-off hero gradient can score high on frequency (500+ elements on one page) and leak into L2 despite 12.5% page coverage. Coverage-based election makes page-level prevalence the PRIMARY gate, directly preventing one-off campaign gradients from being presented as system tokens that the AI then builds interpretive prose around.

**[P0] DTCG $type/$value token schema (Style Dictionary, Diez, Terrazzo, W3C DTCG 2025.10)** — Every token has mandatory $type (color, typography, shadow, etc.) and $value. This creates structural provenance: a prose claim about 'depth' must reference a shadow or gradient token with $type: shadow — it cannot be an untyped narrative invention. The current tokens.json shape uses flat arrays (colorTokens[], shadowTokens[], radiusTokens[]) without typed cross-references. A claim like 'gradient-as-depth replaces shadow elevation' could reference a gradient hex and a shadow hex from DIFFERENT arrays with no structural link proving the claimed relationship. DTCG's type system would force the claim to anchor to specific token paths (shadows.elevation.medium.$value, gradients.hero.$value), making fabrication of false relationships auditable because each $type has defined semantics.

**[P1] Deterministic section-schema gating with evidence quotas (Open Design, DesignMD)** — Open Design uses a deterministic 9-section re-render schema. DesignMD has a 9-section markdown schema with AI-agent-ready prompts that constrain what each section may contain. The current format spec (design_md_format.md) defines 17 sections but the writing_style_guide.md Section 13 (Section Structure Checklist) sets soft 'Density Targets' (e.g., '6-10 values in 4 paragraphs') without enforcement. A section can have 0 token values and still pass all validation checks. Open Design's deterministic re-render means every section has a contract — the renderer produces the same output for the same input because the schema itself constrains what prose is permissible. Making section contracts HARD (required value counts, required token references per paragraph) would make fabrication structurally impossible because ungrounded prose would fail the section contract.

**[P1] Thin-evidence omission / unused-style filtering (Superposition, CSS Stats occurrence clustering)** — Superposition analyzes computed CSS + DOM and EXCLUDES unused styles. CSS Stats clusters colors by OCCURRENCE, implicitly filtering noise. The shared pattern: when evidence is thin, the tool OMITS rather than fabricating. sk-design-md-generator's writing_style_guide.md Section 16 (Named Principle Pattern) requires naming a design principle for every major decision, even when the data is sparse (e.g., 2 shadow tokens, one of which is the default browser shadow). AP-25 (Unnamed Principles) treats MISSING a principle name as a defect. This creates an obligate-fabrication pressure: the AI MUST name principles even when evidence is insufficient. The external tools show that omission is the correct anti-fabrication posture.

**[P2] Explicit component hierarchy mapping (Builder.io Visual Copilot, Anima, Locofy)** — These design-to-code tools work via explicit hierarchy/component MAPPING rather than freeform interpretation. Every visual element maps to a specific component in a specific hierarchy — 'this shadow belongs to this card component, which has this variant, at this position in the tree.' sk-design-md-generator's cluster.ts already identifies components (Button, Input, Card, etc.) and captures their styles with interaction states, but the WRITE phase prose treats design characteristics as abstract system properties rather than component-grounded facts. The 'gradient-as-depth' fabrication would be impossible if every depth claim had to cite: 'Component X uses shadow Y at elevation Z, and no component uses gradient-based depth — depth is shadow-only (0 gradient-depth tokens across N components).'

Evidence: Builder.io Visual Copilot / Anima / Locofy: design→code via explicit hierarchy/component MAPPING (not regeneration). Catalog notes: 'explicit component mapping; spec-driven export.' The mapping pattern forces claims to anchor to concrete, enumerable entities.

Evidence: design-extract (github Manavarya09, 3.3k stars): multi-URL synthesis with coverage-based token election, DTCG tokens, WCAG audit, MCP server. Mentioned in project catalog as 'ELECTS tokens by COVERAGE (site-wide vs one-off).'

Evidence: Open Design (nexu-io, 57.4k stars): DESIGN.md 9-section schema with deterministic re-render. DesignMD (designmd.cc): URL -> DESIGN.md via headless browser, live @media + interaction states, 9-section markdown schema with AI-agent-ready prompts.

Evidence: Superposition: renders page, analyzes computed CSS + DOM, EXCLUDES unused styles, converts to tokens. CSS Stats: computed-style introspection; color clustering by OCCURRENCE; specificity graphs. Both demonstrate that filtering by actual presence is the correct anti-fabrication pattern.

Evidence: W3C DTCG 2025.10 specification with 20+ reference implementations. Style Dictionary, Diez, Terrazzo all enforce $type/$value as zero-invention transformation frameworks. design-extract outputs DTCG tokens natively.

Recommendation: In cluster.ts classifyColorStability(), make pageCoverage the dominant gate: tokens with <30% page coverage are capped at L3 regardless of other scores. Add a pageDistribution [perUrl] frequency map to ColorToken. In the WRITE phase prompt template, add 'Only tokens present on ≥30% of crawled pages may be described as system conventions.' The VALIDATE phase should add a coverage-gating check similar to checkStabilityGating that flags any hex in DESIGN.md whose source token has pageCoverage <0.3.

Recommendation: In design_md_format.md, convert 'Density Target' from soft guidance to hard quotas per section: Atmosphere requires ≥6 token values, Key Characteristics require ≥2 values per item, Shadow Philosophy requires ≥3 shadow token references, etc. In validate.ts, add a checkEvidenceQuotas function that counts token references per section and fails sections that don't meet minimums. This is complementary to DTCG provenance — quotas check quantity, provenance checks relationship accuracy.

Recommendation: In the EXTRACT phase, emit a second output file tokens.dtcg.json alongside tokens.json using the W3C DTCG 2025.10 $type/$value schema. In the WRITE phase prompt, add 'Every named design principle (chromatic depth, shadow-as-border, etc.) must be accompanied by the DTCG token path(s) that constitute its evidence.' In the VALIDATE phase, add a dtcgProvenance check that parses DESIGN.md for named principles, extracts the claimed token paths, and verifies each exists in tokens.dtcg.json with the correct $type. Cross-references between $types (e.g., 'gradient' replacing 'shadow') that don't exist in the data become validation failures.

Recommendation: In the WRITE phase, add a rule: 'Every claim about a design system pattern must cite at least one specific component and its token values as evidence. Abstract claims without component grounding are fabrication.' In validate.ts, extend section coverage checks to verify that prose claims in the Depth & Elevation section cite componentIDs that exist in tokens.components. A 'shadow philosophy' claim about how the system uses depth should reference the shadow values in specific ComponentVariant styles.

Recommendation: In writing_style_guide.md Section 16, add a gate: 'A named principle requires at minimum 3 distinct token instances across at least 2 crawled pages. If fewer than 3 tokens of a given type (shadow, gradient, radius) exist, write "Insufficient data to identify a [type] system — only N tokens extracted." instead of inventing a principle.' In the WRITE prompt template, add: 'If a design concept cannot be traced to at least 3 concrete tokens in tokens.json, do not describe it as a system characteristic.' In validate.ts, add a thinEvidence check that counts token categories mentioned in DESIGN.md principles and flags any that have <3 backing tokens.

**[P0] A10: single score conflates value fidelity with prose provenance** — validateDesignMd (line 347) computes score = 100 - failures*5 - warnings*1. A DESIGN.md with all real hexes, complete sections, and proper format — but prose full of 'A sleek, modern design system with elegant depth principles' — scores 99/100 and passes (≥80, no critical failures). The score gives no signal that the prose is unverified filler. There is no mechanical check that prose claims (color roles, typography rationale, depth philosophy) trace back to actual token data. The prose-provenance gap is the primary hallucination vector the validator misses.

**[P0] A6: stability-gating blind to non-color tokens** — checkStabilityGating (validate.ts:100-123) only iterates tokens.colorTokens for L4 detection. ShadowToken (types.ts:448), RadiusToken (455), ComponentGroup (461), and TypographyLevel (440) all carry stability?:StabilityClassification with layer:'content' possible, but the validator never checks them. A fabricated 'layered depth system' built on a one-off image-derived shadow (L4) passes validation because the shadow's CSS string is real — checkPhantomColors doesn't trace shadow strings at all, and checkStabilityGating ignores shadows. Gradients (types.ts:394-399) lack stability fields entirely despite cluster.ts classifying color stability for gradient-sourced colors.

**[P0] A7: no code enforces banned-word / interpretive-adjective discipline** — quality_checklist.md DQ-01 (line 193) lists 14 banned words: sleek, modern, clean, minimalist, beautiful, stunning, elegant, seamless, intuitive, cutting-edge, state-of-the-art, leverage, utilize, robust. anti_patterns.md AP-02 (line 62) adds: professional, polished, sophisticated. AP-09 (line 233) adds filler phrases: 'Let's dive into', 'Moving on to', 'Now let's look at', 'As we can see', 'It's worth noting'. NO validate.ts function checks any of these. The validator passes prose like 'A clean, modern design with elegant typography' with zero friction.

**[P1] A12: checkUnknownFonts permissive skip-list masks fabricated fonts** — checkUnknownFonts (validate.ts:125-178) has 15+ skip heuristics. Line 171 has an explicit allowlist of 'common system fonts not always in extraction': Georgia, Times New Roman, Arial, Helvetica, Verdana, Courier, Inter, Fira Code, JetBrains Mono, DM Sans, Noto Sans, Roboto, system-ui. A doc that claims 'Typography uses Helvetica Neue for headings' would pass if 'Helvetica' is in the skip-list — the 'Neue' suffix would still be caught, but 'Helvetica' alone would not. More critically, the 15 heuristic skips (no uppercase → skip, looks like CSS → skip, camelCase → skip, ALL-CAPS → skip, quoted → skip, underscore → skip, comma-separated → skip) create a wide bypass surface. A fabricated font like 'Source Serif Pro' would NOT be caught if it appeared without backticks. However, the actual risk is moderate: the heuristic requires backtick wrapping AND uppercase letters AND reasonable length, so random fabrication is caught. The real gap is that the skip-list at line 171 treats 'Georgia' as always-valid even when the extracted data has zero Georgia usage.

Evidence: quality_checklist.md:192-195 (DQ-01), anti_patterns.md:46-62 (AP-02), anti_patterns.md:233-234 (AP-09 filler), validate.ts:287-310 (checkContent exists but only checks typography table and color count)

Evidence: validate.ts:100-123 (checkStabilityGating loop), types.ts:443-462 (stability fields on Shadow/Radius/Component), types.ts:394-399 (gradients missing stability), cluster.ts:539-563 (classifyShadowStability/classifyRadiusStability/classifyComponentStability exist)
Evidence: validate.ts:125-178 (full checkUnknownFonts), validate.ts:171 (explicit system font skip-list), types.ts:362-367 (fontInfo structure with fontFaces + loadedFonts)

**[P0] B10 — Frequency underweighted; L4/one-off colors can score into L2** — CONFIRMED with mechanism. The score ceiling per signal is: pagesCoverage +30, text/border +25, multi-dim +15, cssVar +20, frequency +20, achromatic +10 = 120 max. Frequency contributes at most ±20 (lines 419–421), while pagesCoverage + cssVar + usage-type alone can contribute 75 points. A one-off content-derived color (e.g., a hero gradient stop bound to --hero-accent) that: (a) appears on ≥50% of pages because its parent section is shared → +20 pagesCoverage, (b) is mapped to a CSS variable → +20, (c) is used as text → +25, (d) has frequency ≤5 → −20. Total = 45 ≥ 30 → classified L2 (system). The SKILL.md contract (lines 275–278) requires L4 = 'excluded entirely' but this token gets included in main DESIGN.md sections as a 'system' token, enabling fabrication from a content-derived value. The frequency penalty window (−20 for ≤5, 0 for 6–49, +10 for 50–499) is too narrow relative to the other signals.

**[P0] B11b — Hard RGB Destructive heuristic is fragile and false-positives on red brand colors** — CONFIRMED with mechanism. Line 1245: `bg.r > 180 && bg.g < 100 && bg.b < 100` detects only red-spectrum backgrounds as 'Destructive'. Failure modes: (a) Non-red destructive colors (amber #EAB308 → r=234>180,g=179≥100 → NOT caught; orange #EA580C → r=234>180,g=88<100,b=12<100 → caught, but both are orange-ish), (b) Sites where red IS the primary brand color — ALL red buttons get classified as Destructive, even when they are the primary CTA. The heuristic cannot distinguish semantic intent from color alone. (c) Dark red destructive buttons (#991B1B: r=153<180 → not caught). (d) No check for text color or border — a red-bg Card component (not a button) would also get Destructive classification on its variant.

**[P0] B3 — deltaE<3 under-clusters brand colors, corrupts stability signals** — CONFIRMED with mechanism. The deltaE threshold of 3 in ×100-scaled OKLCH (lines 348–354, 708) is equivalent to an unscaled perceptual difference <0.03 — far tighter than the 1–3 DE2000 range for just-noticeable difference. A brand-blue pair #3B82F6 (L≈0.66,C≈0.22,h≈260) and its lighter scale variant #60A5FA (L≈0.73,C≈0.18,h≈260) yields deltaE≈8.1 and will NOT merge. The greedy single-pass loop (lines 703–724) sorts by frequency and commits to the first match, with no iterative re-centering, so once split, a brand color stays split. Each fragment gets diluted frequency and pagesCoverage, pulling its stability score below the L1 cutoff (≥60) even though the unified token would qualify. The per-page frequency is also divided (`Math.round(c.frequency / c.pages.size)` at line 741), further degrading the classification.

**[P1] B10b — No boundary-disambiguation; L2/L3 cliff misclassifies borderline tokens** — CONFIRMED. The thresholds at lines 429–432 are hard cutoffs: score 30 → L2, score 29 → L3. The SKILL.md contract line 309 says 'ESCALATE IF a token straddles the L2/L3 boundary and the classifier cannot disambiguate.' No escalation or boundary-aware logic exists — a score of 29 produces L3 with confidence 0.69, while 30 produces L2 with confidence 0.70, an identical confidence band across a classification cliff. The confidence normalization (line 434) maps score −40..+60 to 0..1 linearly, so confidences near thresholds are always ~0.5–0.7 and never trigger a 'low confidence → escalate' path.

**[P1] B11 — classifyVariant default-to-Primary masks real variant diversity** — CONFIRMED with mechanism. The function at lines 1234–1257 has only 4 classification branches: Ghost (transparent bg), Destructive (hard RGB red check), Primary (dark bg + light text), Secondary (light bg + dark text + shadow/border). The final `return 'Primary'` (line 1256) is a catch-all fallthrough that captures: (a) light bg + light text without shadow/border → 'Primary' (should be Ghost-on-light or Outline), (b) medium-brightness bg (0.3 ≤ L ≤ 0.7) with any text → 'Primary', (c) dark bg + dark text → falls through past the Primary check (needs textLum>0.5) → hits default 'Primary'. This flattens 3+ real variant classes (Outline, Tertiary, Link, Subtle) into 'Primary', causing the downstream DESIGN.md to over-report Primary variants and under-report or fabricate missing ones.

**[P1] B12 — Component geometric heuristics are site-specific and will fail for common designs** — CONFIRMED with mechanism. Badge heuristic (line 1191): `radiusPx>=100` requires fully-pill border-radius — misses badges with radius 8–24px (standard pill badge on smaller elements), `height<30` misses 32–40px status badges, `hasBg` misses border-only badge styles. Card heuristic (lines 1204–1214): `width 200–800` excludes sidebar cards (<200px) and full-width dashboard cards (>800px), `padding>=12` excludes compact card designs (padding 8–10px common in dense UIs), `childrenCount>=2` is the most fragile — a simple card `

Text

` has childrenCount=1 and will be skipped entirely, missing the most common card pattern. The `radiusPx>0` gate excludes sharp-corner card designs (common in brutalist/material-flat systems). These thresholds are tuned for a specific design density and break across different information-architecture choices.

**[P1] B3s — Primary-sort greed corrupts cluster representativeness** — CONFIRMED. The clustering loop (lines 701–724) sorts by frequency descending then greedily assigns each color to the first representative within deltaE<3. The first-seen high-frequency color becomes the permanent centroid for its region. If a later-seen color has higher perceptual quality (higher chroma, more typical of the cluster mean) it cannot become the representative. This means the cluster's hex/OKLCH values in the output ColorToken (line 739–753) are those of the highest-frequency member, which may not be the best exemplar. The downstream DESIGN.md displays this hex as the canonical value for that color role.

**[P2] B12b — Double-defined classifyShadow function masks the exported version** — CONFIRMED. `classifyShadow` is defined twice: an exported version at line 374 and a shadowing local function at line 1047 inside `clusterTokens`. The local version is identical in logic but shadows the exported one within the function scope. No downstream caller inside `clusterTokens` can reach the exported version, making the export dead code for the single-extraction path. The merge path (`mergeTokenSets` at line 1648) does NOT call either classifyShadow — it merges pre-classified ShadowTokens by exact value match only. This is low-severity but indicates code that evolved without cleanup.

**[P2] B4 — Hue circular-distance formula is correct; modulo is harmless dead code** — REFUTED. The expression `Math.abs(color.h - other.h) % 360` followed by `if (hueDist > 180) hueDist = 360 - hueDist` at lines 781–783 correctly computes circular distance for all h values in [0,360]. Example: h=350 vs h=10 → Math.abs(340)%360=340 → 340>180 → 360−340=20 ✓. The `% 360` is redundant since Math.abs output ≤360, but harmless. The main deltaE function (line 351–352) uses `Math.sin(dhRad/2)` which is also circularly correct for any angle. No failure case exists for the stated inputs.

**[P0] C1: missing anti-fabrication clause in SKILL.md** — SKILL.md §4 NEVER rules (lines 297-304) only prohibit numeric-value fabrication: 'NEVER estimate, round, or invent a hex, pixel, font-weight, shadow, or radius value.' The ALWAYS rules only mandate copying numeric CSS values verbatim. There is ZERO language prohibiting the model from asserting design relationships, causal claims, philosophical interpretations, named principles, or comparative claims about other systems — all of which are equally hallucinatory and equally unfalsifiable against tokens.json. The Cardinal Fidelity Rule (line 240) covers 'every hex, pixel, font-weight, box shadow...' — purely quantitative. Nothing covers qualitative assertions.

**[P0] C11: style-guide/format-spec contradiction** — Style guide §10 Information Density (line 347) states: 'A paragraph with zero values is an opinion. A paragraph with one value is an observation. A paragraph with 2-4 values is documentation.' This is the 'deletion test' — prose without measurable values is opinion and should be deleted. The format spec (design_md_format.md) simultaneously MANDATES philosophy paragraphs (Depth §9, Motion §10, Visual Theme §1 named principles) that routinely contain zero token-extractable values — they are pure narrative. The result is a direct contradiction: the format spec requires paragraphs that the style guide classifies as 'opinion' and instructs the model to delete.

**[P0] C3: mandatory-philosophy solicits fabrication** — Three format-spec clauses demand named philosophy/narrative regardless of whether tokens.json supplies evidence: (a) §9 Depth & Elevation requires 'Named principle -- state the depth philosophy upfront using one of these categories' (line 542) unconditionally; (b) §10 Motion System is REQUIRED even when no motion data exists — the minimal fallback STILL demands a 'Motion philosophy' sentence (line 589-592); (c) §1 Visual Theme & Atmosphere requires 2-4 'Named design principles' (line 122-128) plus 'Comparative framing -- at least one sentence in the prose must use a comparative structure' (line 130). With zero tokens backing a 'chromatic depth' or 'whispered authority' claim, the model MUST fabricate to satisfy the mandatory clause.

**[P1] C10: escalate-if and stamp-ABSENT for missing backing tokens** — Currently SKILL.md §4 ESCALATE IF (lines 305-311) covers extract failures, dark-mode ambiguity, L2/L3 boundary straddlers, and extractor-bug hex mismatches — but has NO path for when required philosophy/narrative sections have zero backing token data. The prompt template (design_md_prompt_template.md §2) also has no 'if absent, stamp ABSENT' fallback. The result: the model fabricates because saying 'I have no data for this required section' is not a supported output path.

**[P1] C2: writing_style_guide.md is the primary fabrication driver** — The writing_style_guide.md is ALWAYS-loaded (SKILL.md §2 Resource Loading Levels table, line 103). It is the most dangerous resource for fabrication because four sections actively solicit interpretive invention beyond tokens.json: §15 Comparative Framing Pattern (lines 529-586) provides sentence templates for claiming what 'most systems' / 'the conventional approach' does — knowledge the model does not have from a single-site crawl; §16 Named Principle Pattern (lines 589-631) declares 'Unnamed decisions get forgotten' and provides a naming formula with 10+ examples, encouraging the model to assign evocative names to patterns it merely observes; §17 Frequency Interpretation Pattern (lines 634-661) frames raw frequency as 'noise' and solicits 'narrative of intent' — i.e., fabricate WHY; §18 Intent Narration Pattern (lines 664-691) explicitly bridges 'the gap between WHAT a system does and WHY' — the WHY cannot be extracted from CSS.

**[P1] C9: AP-29 Interpretive Fabrication anti-pattern** — New anti-pattern: asserting a design relationship, cause, design intent, or qualitative consistency that is not present in the raw tokens.json but is invented from the model's training data or the style guide's narrative-solicitation patterns. Two canonical examples: (1) Gradient-depth: tokens.json records a gradient value `linear-gradient(135deg, #1a1a2e, #16213e)` but the model writes 'The gradient creates atmospheric depth by transitioning from cool navy to deeper midnight tones' — the gradient endpoints exist in tokens, but the claim that it 'creates depth' or the description 'atmospheric' / 'navy' / 'midnight' is fabrication. (2) Focus-consistency: tokens.json records `outline: 2px solid #533afd` on buttons but the model writes 'The consistent 2px focus ring across all interactive elements ensures a unified accessibility experience' — the value exists but the claim of cross-element consistency and the intent ('ensures unified experience') is not verifiable from the data.
